channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Together they've been in office more than 70 years. Now their races could help swing House control
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One is the longest-serving woman in congressional history. The other is dean of the Republican delegation from California and the chairman of a subcommittee responsible for Pentagon spending. Together, they have more than seven decades of experience serving in Congress.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five takeaways from a fiery Wisconsin Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) and Republican challenger Eric Hovde squared off on a debate stage Friday evening in Madison in a fiery exchange of attacks that showed how intense this race has become after largely flying under the radar for months.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A failed mic leaves Donald Trump pacing the stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donald Trump paced his rally stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes Friday night in Detroit after his microphone cut out.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris mocks pro-life protesters 'at the wrong rally' hours before Catholic charity dinner snub
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday told pro-life protesters they were at the "wrong rally" as she campaigned in Wisconsin and skipped the Al Smith Catholic charity dinner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: After Roberson ruling, upcoming Court of Criminal Appeals election in the spotlight
item.description: Three of the five judges who allowed Robert Roberson’s execution to proceed will be leaving the court after Paxton-backed primary challenges.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden's gone silent on Ukraine support, ranking member of Armed Services Committee warns
item.description: Roger Wicker, the head of the Senate’s armed forces committee, urged President Biden to aggressively accelerate aid to Ukraine in his final weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Republicans demand answers about Venezuelan gang members
item.description: White House spokesperson Angelo Fernández Hernández has called reports Biden is "secretly flying" people into the U.S. “categorically false.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis admin blocked from threatening TV stations over pro-abortion ads
item.description: A Florida judge temporarily halted the Florida state government from threatening to take legal measures against television stations over pro-abortion ads the government calls "false.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' during Harris interview
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said former President Donald Trump's comments about "the enemy within" was mistakenly absent from a clip shown.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: House Dems sue FEC accusing GOP of exploiting campaign loophole
item.description: The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, the House Democrats campaign arm, filed its lawsuit against the FEC on Thursday, alleging Republicans are using a campaign finance loophole in their television ads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Submit your questions for the JD Vance town hall on NewsNation
item.description: X users can use the hashtag #VanceTownHall to submit questions to JD Vance, moderated by Chris Cuomo.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: 25 Pinellas County voting locations change after hurricanes
item.description: Twenty-five polling locations in Pinellas County have been changed due to hurricanes Helene and Milton, according to the county supervisor of elections.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5
